commit.message:
Add CI check to verify dist/ is up to date (#13)

Adds a dist-check job that runs 'make dist' and fails if the generated
dist/mcp-launcher.yaml differs from what is committed, preventing stale
dist artifacts from causing runtime errors like CRD validation failures.

Signed-off-by: Matthias Wessendorf <mwessend@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[CUR-982] Fix cross-session auth collision causing wrong role display (#459)

## Summary
- Detect cross-tab Firebase auth collision by tracking the session UID
from `signIn()` in a new `_sessionUid` field
- When `authStateChanges` fires with a different UID (another browser
tab signed in), sign out instead of silently adopting the foreign user's
identity
- Prevents wrong role badge display and addresses FDA 21 CFR Part 11
authority check concerns

## Changes
- **`auth_service.dart`**: Added `_sessionUid` field, set on sign-in,
cleared on sign-out. `_init()` listener compares incoming UID against
`_sessionUid` and calls `signOut()` on mismatch.
- **`auth_service_test.dart`**: New test group simulating cross-tab
collision via `MockFirebaseAuth.stateChangedStreamController`

## Test plan
- [x] New unit test: pushes a different `MockUser` UID onto
`authStateChanges` stream while Admin is signed in → verifies
`isAuthenticated` becomes false and `currentUser` is null
- [x] All 52 existing auth service tests pass
- [ ] Manual: open two browser windows, sign in as Admin in one and
Study Coordinator in the other — verify the first window signs out
instead of showing wrong role

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix French TTS in Chrome by explicitly selecting a matching voice

Chrome requires an explicit voice object to reliably use non-default languages.
Load voices on startup (and on voiceschanged) then pick the best match by
language code when speaking.

Brain admin: fix 'no brain' error — use getLocId on all channel endpoints

Admin requests set req.locationId = '__shared__', so endpoints using
req.locationId directly fail to find brains stored under real locationIds.

Fixed endpoints (now all use getLocId to resolve correct locationId):
- POST   /:brainId/channels              (add channel)
- POST   /:brainId/channels/:id/queue    (refresh / sync channel)
- POST   /:brainId/channels/:id/sync     (background sync)
- DELETE /:brainId/channels/:id          (remove channel)

commit.message:
fix: resolver diagnosis heredoc syntax error in GHA workflow

The heredoc with unindented JS content broke YAML parsing, causing
dedup-teams to exit code 2 (syntax error). Replaced with single-line
node -e to avoid heredoc indentation issues in YAML run blocks.

Also caused generate-dashboard to not run (cascading failure).

commit.message:
fix: create skeleton ~/.zshrc for admin and root on activation

NixOS does not generate per-user .zshrc files — zsh config lands in
/etc/zshrc. Create a minimal .zshrc skeleton via activationScripts so
users find the expected file on first login. The file is never
overwritten so users can edit it freely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(admin): move useMaterialReactTable hook before conditional return

Fixes React hooks rules-of-hooks lint error that broke the Vercel build.
The hook was called after an early return for mobile, violating React's
rules that hooks must be called in the same order every render.

commit.message:
feat: Sprint 3 — scheduling, appointments, waiting room, calendar UI

Backend (S-018/S-019/S-020):
- ScheduleConfig model: slot duration, working days/hours, lunch break, max simultaneous
- Appointment model: UUID PK, status/type/source choices, conflict detection via clean()
- Migration 0002_appointment_scheduleconfig
- AppointmentSerializer + ScheduleConfigSerializer
- AppointmentViewSet: list/create/update with today action + PATCH status endpoint
- ScheduleConfigViewSet (admin only)
- AvailableSlotsView: GET /professionals/{id}/available-slots?date=&duration=
- WaitingRoomView: GET /waiting-room (today's scheduled/confirmed/waiting)
- URLs: appointments, schedule-configs, available-slots, waiting-room

Frontend:
- /appointments — weekly calendar grid (08h-18h × 7 days), color-coded by status,
  click empty slot → AppointmentModal prefilled, click appointment → detail panel + status change
- /waiting-room — real-time list with 30s auto-refresh, counters (waiting/in-progress/done),
  quick actions: Chamar → in_progress, Concluir, Faltou → no_show
- AppointmentModal — patient autocomplete, professional select, date picker, available slots
  grid, type selector, notes, 409 TIME_SLOT_UNAVAILABLE handling
- DashboardShell: add Agenda (/appointments) and Sala de Espera (/waiting-room) to nav

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: rewrite Spotify API to use GraphQL Partner API

The spclient approach (apresolve.spotify.com) returned 404 on ALL endpoints.
The public REST API (api.spotify.com/v1) permanently 429-blocks sp_dc tokens.

Switch to the GraphQL Partner API (api-partner.spotify.com/pathfinder/v1/query)
which is the same backend the official Spotify web player uses. This is the
ONLY confirmed working approach for sp_dc-derived access tokens.

Changes:
- SpotifyAuthConfig: add GraphQL endpoint, client version, persisted query hashes
- SpotifyAuthManager: add getClientToken() for client token acquisition, pack
  clientId into ServiceToken.scope alongside username
- TokenManager: add getSpotifyClientId() interface method
- TokenManagerImpl: implement getSpotifyClientId(), update scope parsing for
  new "username|clientId" format
- SpotifyApiClient: complete rewrite from spclient to GraphQL Partner API
  using libraryV3 and fetchPlaylist persisted queries
- SpotifyDataModule: inject SpotifyAuthManager into SpotifyApiClient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Chat: run audit on any URL mentioned by user, not just current tab

- Extract URL from user message with regex
- Expanded intent: "run", "analyze", "check", "scan" + any URL triggers audit
- auditPage() called on the mentioned URL (or current tab URL as fallback)
- Keyword/schema tools also use the extracted URL
- System prompt updated: AI now knows it CAN run live audits on any URL
- Removed wrong "I don't have ability to run audits" behaviour

commit.message:
fix: rename default user mynixos -> admin to match Contabo expectation

Contabo's cloud-init likely targets a user named "admin" for password
and SSH key injection. Renaming aligns the default_user in both
system_info (contabo.nix) and the NixOS user definition (user.nix).

Captured Theresa Bailey's design preferences from Mar 24 email thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add init, db-migrate, api-contract, status skills + testing/git workflow questions

New skills:
- /init: Scaffold project from stack config (create-next-app, venv, docker-compose,
  install deps, setup auth/i18n/AI, create CLAUDE.md, first commit)

commit.message:
Replace homemade template engine with Vite, add deploy tooling (#3)

- Add Vite frontend in client/ (vanilla HTML/JS, no React) with multi-page
  build for preview and crop-tester pages
- Refactor Express into modular routes (src/routes/, src/middleware/)
  and pure API server; client fetches metadata via /preview/meta JSON endpoint
- Serve Vite build under /{PATH_SECRET}/preview/ in production so admin UI
  stays gated behind the secret (client derives base from URL path, no secret
  baked into the JS bundle)
- Align CLI scripts with vet-pdf-corrector: dev, build, deploy:prod, logs:prod,
  install:all, clean (using concurrently for dev)
- Add deploy.sh for one-command SSH deployment with healthcheck polling
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (client build → server deps → production image)
- Port handling follows API_PORT/VITE_PORT pattern for Conductor compatibility
- Fix countdown to show hours (rounded) when ≤3 days remain

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Design overhaul: colors, cards, sidebar, nav, tags, hover effects

- Background #f8fafc, unified CSS color variables
- Cards: white bg, #e2e8f0 border, 14px radius, 0 10px 25px shadow
- Card hover: translateY(-3px) + stronger shadow (0.2s ease)
- Card titles: #1e40af, font-weight 600, blue divider
- Header: white + thin border (Option A), fixed/sticky
- Sidebar: #f1f5f9, right border + soft shadow
- Nav active link: #2563eb color + 2px bottom border (auto-detected)
- Keywords (CAD/CAM/AI): #dbeafe bg, #1d4ed8 text, 6px radius
- Links: primary #2563eb / hover accent #0ea5e9

commit.message:
fix: restore monthly accumulation with actual cycle count denominator

Previous commit over-simplified to just current week. Now:
- Accumulates weekly peaks across billing cycle resets (as before)
- Divides by actual number of cycles seen, NOT hardcoded /4
- First-week users: divided by 1 (shows full weekly value)
- 4-week users: divided by 4 (proper monthly average)
- Multi-plan: per-plan split still works

スポーツ誤認防止のため、冒頭に「壁打ち＝思考の整理・内省のための対話」の定義を明記。物理的ボール・スポーツコーチとしての解釈を絶対禁止。

feat: batch 1+2 features — canned notes, preferred channel, stock-linked parts, repeat service

Features implemented:
- Canned diagnosis notes: CRUD + 15 pre-built defaults covering brakes,
  engine, suspension, tires, electrical, body, exhaust, HVAC
- Customer preferred channel: whatsapp/email/app/sms on customer record,
  auto-select when sending estimates
- Stock-linked parts: adding parts to job deducts stock + records inventory
  adjustment. Deleting parts returns stock. Matched by part_number.

  last completed job with labour + parts lines for one-click repeat
- Preferred channel added to customer validator and service

commit.message:
Fix AI summary generation silently failing on YouTube videos

- VideoFeedItem: add catch block to handleGetCaption; show error
  message inline below the button instead of silently resetting
- api.py: convert get_caption, fetch_news, fetch_videos to async
  handlers using asyncio.to_thread for all blocking I/O (transcript
  fetch, LLM call, RSS fetch, channel resolve) — prevents the server
  thread from being blocked during long-running requests
